claim: Lupus-prone mice infected with curliproducing bacteria have higher autoantibody titers compared to controls.

result.claim: Lupus-prone mice infected with curliproducing bacteria have higher autoantibody titers compared to controls.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by direct evidence. Proposition ID prop_1443 explicitly states, "Curli-producing bacteria infection triggered higher autoantibody titers in lupus-prone mice." This proposition originates from paper ID 1834762, titled "Amyloid-DNA Composites of Bacterial Biofilms Stimulate Autoimmunity." The abstract of this paper further corroborates the claim, stating, "We also found that the infection of lupus-prone mice with curli-producing bacteria triggered higher autoantibody titers compared to curli-deficient bacteria." This provides clear and consistent evidence directly addressing the claim.

evidence.text: IgG2a and IgG2b production were increased in the gp130F759/F759 mice.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice displayed splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Increased disease activity and active lupus nephritis are associated with elevated serum IgE, self-reactive IgEs and activated basophils in individuals with SLE.
evidence.source: BASOPHILS AND THE T HELPER 2 ENVIRONMENT CAN PROMOTE THE DEVELOPMENT OF LUPUS NEPHRITIS

evidence.text: Autoimmune disease susceptibility and resistance alleles on mouse chromosome 3 (Idd3) correlate with differential expression of the key immunoregulatory cytokine interleukin-2 (IL-2).
evidence.source: Interleukin-2 gene variation impairs regulatory T cell function and causes autoimmunity

evidence.text: Marked increase of inflammatory cells were detected in cold-acclimated ApoE(-/-) mice.

evidence.text: F11r(-/-)Rag1(-/-) mice exhibited increased microflora-dependent colitis.

evidence.text: IFN-gammaR(-/-) mice showed persistent disease.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Expansion of host macrophages prevented the development of alveolar proteinosis in mice transplanted with GM-Csf-receptor-deficient progenitors.
evidence.source: Tissue-resident macrophages self-maintain locally throughout adult life with minimal contribution from circulating monocytes.

evidence.text: Levels of primary bile acids and sugar alcohols increase in mice after antibiotic treatment.

evidence.text: IFN-gamma-deficient mice developed fatal autoimmune disease.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Mice fed a low-iron diet were protected from CS-induced COPD.

evidence.text: An elevated presence of neutrophils and macrophages was observed in lesion areas of myeKlf2(-/-)Ldlr(-/-) mice.

evidence.text: These mice developed lymphoblastic leukemia.
